questions.prompt: Which review note would help most for AP Macroeconomics: Open Economy?
questions.correctText: Open Economy matters because it helps explain disciplinary reasoning.
choices: describing a topic without explaining why it matters
memorizing an isolated fact without evidence
Open Economy appears on tests, so no explanation is needed.
Open Economy matters because it helps explain disciplinary reasoning.
questions.explanation: Strong review notes pair the concept with why it matters in the unit.

questions.prompt: A released-test-style item asks students to interpret Open Economy. What should they do first?
questions.correctText: Read the prompt, source, or data for the exact task.
choices: Eliminate every answer that sounds detailed.
ignoring the source or data in the prompt
making a claim without supporting evidence
Read the prompt, source, or data for the exact task.

questions.prompt: Which classroom explanation would best connect Open Economy to the larger course skill?
questions.correctText: Open Economy is useful when students explain reasoning.

disciplinary reasoning should be ignored unless it appears in the title.
Open Economy is useful when students explain reasoning.
